page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture using the Neoverse-N1 core model, this instance features 64 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz with no simultaneous multithreading. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The system runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance, contrasted by weaker single-core execution. The multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmark achieved a strong score of 97,055.96, while single-core performance registered in the average tier at 1,519.28. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient, reaching 3,780.5 GB/sec, though large uncached block reads and writes drop to average levels of 165.41 GB/sec and 78.1 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded tasks show notable limitations, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and text processing. However, multi-threaded operations like software compilation with Clang score in the top tier.

The combination of high memory capacity, dedicated physical cores, and fast local NVMe storage aligns with memory-intensive workloads and database operations. Redis SET benchmarks demonstrate strong performance at 72.91 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is also strong at 45.28 GB/sec. Conversely, the ARM64 architecture lacks AVX, AVX-512, and AMX instruction sets, and exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM inference, such as 2.77 tokens per second for 70B model text generation. The instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out workloads that benefit from high memory density and local NVMe storage without requiring x86-specific vector instructions.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong results in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak, and the architecture lacks AVX or AMX instruction sets. This hardware profile supports memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and caching layers that leverage local NVMe storage, offering high resource density for ARM-native workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 physical cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and strong multi-threaded database performance.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes
Strong multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth benchmark results
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded compilation
